channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Ohtani's walk-off homer in 9th completes rally as Dodgers stun winless Braves 6-5 for 8-0 start
item.description: Shohei Ohtani hit a tiebreaking home run in the ninth inning on his bobblehead night, lifting the unbeaten Los Angeles Dodgers over the winless Atlanta Braves 6-5.
item.dc:creator: Beth Harris, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: San Jose Giants beat Oakland Ballers in Bay Area baseball exhibition, evoking memories of original Battle of the Bay
item.description: Oakland Ballers, San Jose Giants look to fill void of lost Bay Area baseball rivalry with "Battle of the Bay 2.0" in San Jose
item.dc:creator: Christian Babcock, The Mercury News

item.title: Teaching Chicago's hard-working values, basketball legend Tom Kleinschmidt builds a dynasty at DePaul Prep
item.description: DePaul College Prep's boys' basketball team, coached by Tom Kleinschmidt, has won three consecutive IHSA championships, thanks to his hard work, discipline, and respect, which he learned growing up on the city's Northwest Side.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lowe, WGN 9

item.title: Ovechkin scores his 892nd career goal. He needs 2 to tie Gretzky and 3 to break the NHL record
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Alex Ovechkin took another step closer to breaking Wayne Gretzky's NHL record by scoring his 892nd career goal in the Washington Capitals' 5-1 loss at the Carolina Hurricanes on Wednesday night. Ovechkin moved two away from tying Gretzky and three from passing him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Heat extend winning streak to 6 games and snap Celtics' 9-game streak with 124-103 victory
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Tyler Herro had 25 points and nine assists and the Miami Heat extended their season-best winning streak to six games with a 124-103 victory over the Boston Celtics on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: KYLE HIGHTOWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFL star Trey Hendrickson rips Bengals' brass over 'poor' communication during contract negotiations
item.description: Trey Hendrickson took exception to Bengals executive Katie Blackburn's public remarks about contract negotiations. The pass rusher said Blackburn's comments were "disappointing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SafeSport knew of allegations against former cop before hiring him as investigator
item.description: People at the U.S. Center for SafeSport knew a former police officer was the subject of an internal investigation at his former job but hired him anyway, according to details released Wednesday by Sen. Chuck Grassley, who is looking into the matter.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: NBA DEI chief Lesley Slaton stepping down: report
item.description: Lesley Slaton, the NBA's chief DEI officer, will be leaving her position, according to Adweek. The NBA will reportedly hire a replacement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump admin freezes funding to Maine for letting trans athletes in girls sports
item.description: The USDA announced a freeze to Maine's federal funding after the state continuously refused to ban trans athletes from girls sports amid federal pressure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Paul Skenes dominates the Rays on the day GQ publishes profile of him and girlfriend Livvy Dunne
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — The only thing that flustered Paul Skenes on Wednesday was getting asked about his photo spread with girlfriend Livvy Dunne in GQ magazine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Carlsbad native earns spot in WSL Challenger Series
item.description: Reid Van Wagoner, a 15-year-old surfer from Carlsbad, has secured a spot in the 2025 Challenger Series after winning a 10-point ride at Soup Bowl in Barbados.
item.dc:creator: Alysse Dodge, Fox 5

item.title: Nothing but the tooth for the Utah Hockey Club
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The average person has 28 teeth. Most hockey players have fewer. With a chance of getting hit in the mouth with a puck, or a stick, or with a fist, a lot of them have lost a tooth, Or two, Or three.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Carson Kelly becomes 1st Cubs player to hit for the cycle in 32 years in 18-3 rout of A's in West Sacramento
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Carson Kelly had come close to hitting for the cycle, yet that elusive triple kept holding him back. He had just two triples over a 10-year big league career before Monday night. That's when Kelly became the first Cubs player in 32 years to hit for the cycle.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Kanon Catchings enters transfer portal
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - After a disappointing freshman season, BYU forward Kanon Catchings has entered the transfer portal. The highly-touted 4-star recruit came to BYU with high expectations. But injuries and lack of playing time derailed his freshman season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Houston's defense carries Cougars into 7th Final Four with 69-50 March Madness win over Tennessee
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Houston’s relentless, harassing defense had Tennessee's shooters dancing around. Now, the Cougars are two-stepping it back to Texas. L.J.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New Mexico taps UC San Diego's Eric Olen to replace Richard Pitino
item.description: New Mexico hired UC San Diego's Eric Olen on Sunday as its next basketball coach. Olen will replace Richard Pitino, who left to become Xavier's coach on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Walter Clayton Jr. rallies Florida into Final Four with 84-79 March Madness win over Texas Tech
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Coach Todd Golden cut the final strand of the net, waved it to the crowd and performed the famed Gator Chomp. A celebration that seemed completely improbable just a few minutes earlier was in high gear. Florida is heading back to the Final Four. Walter Clayton Jr.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former Green Bay Packer charged for alleged dogfighting
item.description: The standout college football running back played 14 games for the Green and Gold between 1994 and 1995.
item.dc:creator: Nick Witwer, NewsNation

item.title: New San Diego Padres mural in North Park nears completion
item.description: A new San Diego Padres-themed mural in North Park replacing a previous work of public art that had been defaced earlier this year by fans of the rival Los Angeles Dodgers is nearing completion, just in time for opening weekend.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Royal Riot Podcast Exclusive: Ariath Piol talks goals, red cards, and his journey to RSL
item.description: Royal Riot Podcast hosts Hayden and Mattey sit down with Ariath Piol in an exclusive interview where the player opens up about his journey to RSL, the emotions, lessons, and support he has received from the club and more.
item.dc:creator: Royal Riot Podcast, ABC 4
